before.description: Multi-stack monorepo deploying three things in parallel

stacks.name: Static site (custom build)
stacks.role: Main marketing site (EN/AR), built to static HTML at build time, esbuild-bundled JS, pre-gzipped + brotli'd

stacks.name: Next.js 3D app
stacks.role: /suraga-3d route, React Three Fiber + GSAP + WASM (Emscripten, JS fallback), also exported to static

stacks.name: Rust/axum backend
stacks.note: Dormant — no deploy config references it

stacks.name: Capacitor mobile
stacks.role: Wraps the site as a mobile app

problems: Pinned to single region (fra1) — every non-EU visitor hit Frankfurt
Postgres pool max:5 per serverless instance — would exhaust DB connections under load
In-memory rate limiter (Map) that reset on every cold start — didn't actually limit anything
API key check used !== — timing-attack vulnerable
Contact form interpolated raw user input into the email HTML body — email-content injection; no CRLF stripping — SMTP header injection possible
No honeypot, no rate limit on the contact endpoint — spam magnet
Hero image shipped at one fixed size to all devices (no responsive srcset)

security.title: Timing-safe API key compare
security.what: Replaced provided !== apiKey with crypto.timingSafeEqual + constant-time length-mismatch handling. Closes timing-attack vector on /api/verification/*.

security.title: Email injection hardening
security.what: HTML-escape all user input before interpolating into email body; strip CR/LF from header-bound fields (name, email, inquiry); cap field lengths.

security.title: CSP hardened
security.what: Removed 'unsafe-inline' from script-src (now 'self' only — safe because JSON-LD isn't executable). Added frame-ancestors 'none' (clickjacking) + upgrade-insecure-requests.

security.title: Contact anti-spam
security.what: Added hidden honeypot field (website) — bots fill it, server silently 200s and drops; real users never see it. Added rate limiting to the contact endpoint.

scalability.title: Postgres pooler sizing
scalability.what: max:5 → max:2, added idleTimeoutMillis, connectionTimeoutMillis, SSL handling. Stops the 'too many connections' cliff across many serverless instances.

scalability.title: Redis-backed rate limiting
scalability.what: New isAllowed() helper: uses Upstash Redis sliding-window counter (survives cold starts, shared across regions) when UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL is set; falls back to the existing in-memory Map otherwise. In-memory Map now capped at 10k entries to prevent memory exhaustion.

speed.title: Multi-region
speed.what: regions: [fra1] → [fra1, sfo1, iad1, sin1]. Cuts TTFB by 200–400ms for Americas/Asia.

speed.title: Responsive hero images
speed.what: Build now generates 480w + 960w AVIF/WebP variants; <picture> now serves a responsive srcset + sizes. A phone now downloads the 6.8KB AVIF instead of the full-res file.

checks.result: generated cleanly, responsive srcset confirmed in index.html + ar.html

decisions.title: Rust backend — your decision
decisions.detail: src/*.rs + Cargo.toml is a complete duplicate of the Node verification API (same routes: /numbers, /numbers/{id}, /textnow-guide, /health), last touched 3 commits ago, and not referenced by any deploy config. It's dormant. I did not delete it — that's your call.
options: Delete (rm -rf src/ Cargo.toml) — removes a maintenance trap, simplest
Wire it up behind a container (Render/Fly) if you want a non-serverless API
Leave it — but it will drift from the Node API and confuse future readers

envVars.name: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL
envVars.purpose: Activates the Redis rate limiter (otherwise in-memory fallback)

envVars.name: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN
envVars.purpose: Activates the Redis rate limiter (otherwise in-memory fallback)

envVars.name: PGSSL
envVars.purpose: Set to 0 only if running against a local Postgres without SSL

alreadyDone.detail: Already done. The main site ships zero Three.js — confirmed grep three public/index.html public/ar.html → 0 matches. Three.js only loads on /suraga-3d, which is the island.
AVIF+WebP were already generated at build time; the only gap was the missing responsive srcset, now fixed.

needsDesign.detail: Honeypot + rate limiting are in, but if you want stronger anti-bot, this needs a site key.

needsDesign.item: Admin surface for the verification DB

filesChanged.summary: escape, CRLF strip, length caps, honeypot, rate limit

git.note: Tree has 8 modified + 2 untracked PNGs. Ready to review and commit.
